title: Enforcing ProcScript Code Conventions
text: Enforcing ProcScript Code Conventions The ProcScript code formatting feature allows you to correct or improve the code indentation and increase the code readability and understanding. As different organizations may use different coding conventions, the following article explains how you can enforce the usage of the same rules across your organization. Defining rules from a centralized location To set custom formatting rules, you have to define a new subsection in the ASN file named [FORMATTING] . For more details, see [FORMATTING] . Under [FORMATTING] , you can add all rules. If a rule is not defined, the default value is used. The [FORMATTING] rules are best placed in an ASN file that is used for your Project, that is, the default ide.asn file, or your own ASN file. You can also use a separate file that is included from your ASN file. For a separate file option, the file name and extension are not important. The file should contain the [FORMATTING] header and the rules. The file can then be included from any ASN file, using #file , for example - #file Z:\rules\rules.asn , or #file Z:\.policy . Ideally, the formatting rules should reside in a read-only location, so that you can enforce its usage across your organization. Note: We do not recommend including the subsection in the environment of every developer manually, as it can easily lead to errors and makes maintenance difficult. A single ASN file in a network location is easier to maintain and share across developers. Related Links [FORMATTING] The [FORMATTING] section of the assignment file allows you to assign rules and customize the code formatting tool in the Source Editor. Was this topic helpful?
